title: Calliandra salvadorensis
intro: Calliandra salvadorensis, also known as Pink Powder Puff, is a flowering plant species native to Central America, characterized by its vibrant pink flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its ability to reduce oxidative stress and promote tissue repair. The bioactive constituents of Calliandra salvadorensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been identified as the primary compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calliandra salvadorensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
taxonomy: Calliandra salvadorensis is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae, and is commonly known as the Salavador plum or the Pink Powderpuff. It is native to Central America, particularly in countries such as Honduras and El Salvador. The classification of this plant is as follows: genus - Calliandra, subfamily - Faboideae, family - Fabaceae. This classification reflects the plant's relationships with other plants within the pea family. The specific classification of Calliandra salvadorensis highlights its unique characteristics within the Fabaceae family.

constituents: Calliandra salvadorensis contains various active constituents, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant contains flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as tannins and saponins. These active constituents are responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The unique combination of these active constituents makes Calliandra salvadorensis a valuable plant for traditional medicine.
